methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $35M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: SendCutSend
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Remedy Robotics is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by David Bell and Jake Sganga, having raised $35M to date. The broader Robotics & Manufacturing sector includes one public comparable (277810.KQ) and eight private peers, including later-stage companies such as Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable valuation approach here. Using total capital raised ($35M) as a proxy for post-money valuation and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier to estimate Series A-to-next-stage progression, this method implies a valuation of $70M. The peer-median method could not be reliably applied due to insufficient same-stage or tightly comparable datapoints within the narrow peer set—while seven same-sector peers exist, fewer than three met relevant filter criteria for statistical confidence.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $60M to $80.5M, centered on $70M. This single-method triangulation carries meaningful uncertainty around step-up magnitude and stage-transition timing in frontier robotics. Key sensitivities include burn rate trajectory, customer concentration, technical risk in core robotics IP, and near-term fundraising momentum. A broadened peer analysis or late-stage comps would strengthen conviction in the upper or lower bound.
